measures.name: count
measures.comment: Represents the total number of rows in the dataset. Use this measure to count all

dimensions.name: day_of_week_sort
dimensions.expr:
CASE
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Monday' THEN 1
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Tuesday' THEN 2
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Wednesday' THEN 3
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Thursday' THEN 4
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Friday' THEN 5
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Saturday' THEN 6
  WHEN source.day_of_week = 'Sunday' THEN 7
  ELSE 99
END

measures.name: count
measures.comment: Represents the total number of rows in the dataset. Use this measure to count all

measures.name: active_customers
measures.expr: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN source.order_status NOT IN ('cancelled') THEN source.customer_id END)

measures.name: count

datasets.name: 9a3dbed5
queryLines:
SELECT
  *
FROM
  dim_customers
WHERE
  join_date >= :date_start
  AND join_date <= :date_end

datasets.name: 5c9b9662
datasets.displayName: Filtered Orders Within Selected Date Range
queryLines:
SELECT
  *
FROM
  dim_customers
WHERE
  join_date >= :date_start
  AND join_date <= :date_end

config.source:
SELECT
  r.review_id,
  r.order_id,
  r.customer_id,
  r.restaurant_id,
  d.name AS restaurant_name,
  d.city AS city,
  r.rating,
  r.review_text,
  r.sentiment,
  r.issue_delivery,
  r.issue_food_quality,
  r.issue_pricing,
  r.issue_portion_size,
  r.review_timestamp,
  CAST(r.review_timestamp AS DATE) AS review_date
FROM ws_dbxprojectrc.`02_silver`.fact_reviews r LEFT JOIN ws_dbxprojectrc.`02_silver`.dim_restaurant d
  ON r.restaurant_id = d.restaurant_id

measures.name: delivery_issue_count
measures.expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN LOWER(CAST(issue_delivery AS STRING)) = 'true' THEN 1 END)

measures.name: food_quality_issue_count
measures.expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN LOWER(CAST(issue_food_quality AS STRING)) = 'true' THEN 1 END)

measures.name: pricing_issue_count
measures.expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN LOWER(CAST(issue_pricing AS STRING)) = 'true' THEN 1 END)

measures.name: portion_size_issue_count
measures.expr: COUNT(CASE WHEN LOWER(CAST(issue_portion_size AS STRING)) = 'true' THEN 1 END)

config.source:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    r.review_id,
    d.name AS restaurant_name,
    d.city AS city,
    CAST(r.review_timestamp AS DATE) AS review_date,
    r.review_timestamp,
    r.sentiment,
    r.issue_delivery,
    r.issue_food_quality,
    r.issue_pricing,
    r.issue_portion_size
  FROM ws_dbxprojectrc.`02_silver`.fact_reviews r
  LEFT JOIN ws_dbxprojectrc.`02_silver`.dim_restaurant d
    ON r.restaurant_id = d.restaurant_id
) SELECT review_id, restaurant_name, city, review_date, review_timestamp, sentiment, 'Delivery' AS issue_category FROM base WHERE LOWER(CAST(issue_delivery AS STRING)) = 'true' UNION ALL SELECT review_id, restaurant_name, city, review_date, review_timestamp, sentiment, 'Food Quality' AS issue_category FROM base WHERE LOWER(CAST(issue_food_quality AS STRING)) = 'true' UNION ALL SELECT review_id, restaurant_name, city, review_date, review_timestamp, sentiment, 'Pricing' AS issue_category FROM base WHERE LOWER(CAST(issue_pricing AS STRING)) = 'true' UNION ALL SELECT review_id, restaurant_name, city, review_date, review_timestamp, sentiment, 'Portion Size' AS issue_category FROM base WHERE LOWER(CAST(issue_portion_size AS STRING)) = 'true'

datasets.name: recent_review_feed
queryLines:
SELECT
  r.review_id,
  d.name AS restaurant_name,
  d.city,
  r.sentiment,
  r.rating,
  r.review_text,
  r.review_timestamp
FROM
  fact_reviews r
    LEFT JOIN dim_restaurant d
      ON r.restaurant_id = d.restaurant_id
WHERE
  r.sentiment IN ('positive', 'negative')
ORDER BY
  r.review_timestamp DESC
